__comment_execution_defaults__.default_modes_field: execution.default_modes controls which cleaning modes workflow uses when --modes is not provided.
__comment_execution_defaults__.analysis_nproc_field: execution.analysis_nproc can pin the MPI rank count used for one-off replicated/partitioned analysis runs. Leave it null to use the largest configured benchmark process_count greater than 1.
__comment_execution_defaults__.mpi_launcher_field: execution.mpi_launcher sets the default MPI launcher for workflow analysis runs unless --mpi-launcher is passed.
__comment_execution_defaults__.serial: Use serial for the easiest newcomer workflow and for smaller datasets.
__comment_execution_defaults__.replicated: Use replicated when you have a small number of MPI ranks and enough memory for every rank to hold the full dataset.
__comment_execution_defaults__.partitioned: Use partitioned when you have more MPI ranks or larger datasets and want the more scalable time-domain decomposition.
__comment_execution_defaults__.practical_rule_of_thumb: Start in serial, move to replicated for a few ranks, and prefer partitioned as data size or rank count makes full-data duplication less attractive.

__comment_benchmark_defaults__.workflow_default_behavior: The workflow command is analysis-first by default. Set benchmark.enabled_by_default to true or pass --benchmark when you want repeated timing studies.
__comment_benchmark_defaults__.enabled_by_default_field: benchmark.enabled_by_default controls whether workflow runs benchmark timing without an explicit CLI override.
__comment_benchmark_defaults__.repeat_field: benchmark.repeat controls how many repeated timing runs are used for each benchmark measurement.
__comment_benchmark_defaults__.process_counts_field: benchmark.process_counts controls the default MPI rank counts used in benchmark studies.
